description_long: This is a classical-style wall fountain constructed from what appears to be blue-gray marble with white veining, topped by an ornate triangular pediment. The front face features a symmetrical arrangement of seven small, bowl-like basins set in a stepped pattern, designed to allow water to cascade from one to the next. The lower section contains a large, octagonal catch basin with a pronounced rim, collecting water from the upper bowls. Decorative elements include sculpted rosettes and a beaded border beneath the pediment, lending an elegant, historical character. The sides are flat and unadorned, while the back is plain, indicating it is intended to be placed against a wall. The overall design suggests a fixed, monumental fountain suitable for parks, plazas, or courtyards.
description: A blue-gray marble wall fountain with ornate pediment, cascading bowl tiers, and a large octagonal basin. Classical decorative motifs and a stepped bowl arrangement define its elegant appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ornate pediment, decorative motifs, seven cascading bowls, and the large octagonal basin.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the fountain, the protruding bowls, and the thickness of the marble structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, unadorned marble back, indicating wall placement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the profile of the bowls and the layered base.
attribute_ornamentation_details: The fountain features a classical triangular pediment with sculpted rosettes and a beaded border, as well as a series of symmetrically arranged bowl tiers. The marble surface is veined, adding a natural decorative effect.
attribute_basin_structure: The basin is large, octagonal, and projects outward with a thick, defined rim. Above it, seven smaller bowls are arranged in a stepped, cascading pattern for water flow.
attribute_jet_or_spray_pattern: No visible water jets or spray patterns are present; the design suggests water would cascade gently from bowl to bowl.
